fact_verdicts.fact: 1. Action id is gov_action1pa6a6yd7pdaxed9nqkshtvtu7jmqe5c8cpf0ej4lnatjj588qkpsq2x2sz2; action type is TreasuryWithdrawals; title is "Alchemy by Sundial x Charms: Cardano-Native Bitcoin Treasury Protocol"; treasury amount is 10000000000000 lovelace; proposed epoch is 639; expires after epoch 646.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 0.

fact_verdicts.fact: 2. The anchor states the proposal requests 10,000,000 ada and uses a reference value of approximately USD 0.20 per ada for a total planning budget of approximately USD 2.0 million.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor abstract and rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 3. The anchor splits the request into two separated pools: Pool 1, approximately USD 1.0 million, for protocol infrastructure and staged launch liquidity; Pool 2, approximately USD 1.0 million, for delivery, audit, integrations, dashboards, governance reporting, legal/compliance work, and go-to-market execution.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor abstract and rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 4. Pool 1 deployment is described as staged over three months: Month 1 approximately USD 250,000 after audit and launch-readiness review; Month 2 approximately USD 250,000 after public reporting, operational review, and a 30-day grace period; Month 3 approximately USD 500,000 after mint/redeem thresholds, reserve-ratio tracking, growth-rate monitoring, and dashboard performance are confirmed.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Pool 1 staging language in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 5. The anchor states the initial reserve target is approximately USD 800,000 FIRE-side liquidity and approximately USD 200,000 ICE-side liquidity, subject to final optimization based on economic modeling, audit feedback, and launch conditions.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 6. The anchor states each tranche is expected to be purchased at an approximate 4:1 FIRE-to-ICE ratio to move the system toward a 5.0x reserve ratio.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 7. The anchor states launch liquidity will be treasury-supported and treasury-owned, and that all profits, yield, and returns generated by the launch liquidity position will be returned to the Cardano Treasury quarterly, converted into ada through Cardano-native DEXs at commercially reasonable rates, with conversion timing and methodology disclosed in monthly reports.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 8. The anchor states launch liquidity principal can be returned to the Treasury after Alchemy reaches a 30-day time-weighted average TVL of at least USD 60 million, subject to a formal governance proposal for DRep and Constitutional Committee review; if that action does not pass, principal remains in the liquidity position and the return proposal may be resubmitted later.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the principal-return condition in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 9. Pool 2 planned allocation is: USD 250,000 for protocol infrastructure; USD 300,000 for engineering, platform, delivery, monitoring, reporting, and capital-formation support; USD 200,000 for independent security review, audit, and economic modeling; USD 100,000 for public dashboards, wallet flows, DEX adapters, documentation, and composability guides; USD 75,000 for legal, compliance, disclosures, treasury reporting, risk documentation, and launch-readiness review; USD 50,000 for ecosystem education, DRep communications, partner activation, and launch coordination; USD 25,000 for fund administration and accounting.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the itemized Pool 2 allocation in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 10. The anchor states delivery funds may be reallocated between delivery categories as requirements are finalized, but total delivery spend may not exceed the approved delivery budget; the infrastructure and launch liquidity pool remains separate and cannot be used for implementation overruns, general operating expenses, personal compensation, or any purpose outside approved deployment; material reallocations will be disclosed.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor budget restrictions.

fact_verdicts.fact: 11. The anchor states there are no DRep funds or personal compensation to Sundial or Charms principals.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated under treasury protections.

fact_verdicts.fact: 12. The anchor states that if Sundial or Charms receives external investment for Alchemy development, the development portion of this treasury request will roll into additional treasury-supported launch liquidity instead of duplicating compensation.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the rollover commitment.

fact_verdicts.fact: 13. The anchor states the maximum acceptable ADA reference rate is USD 0.35, and if ADA appreciates above that level before or during delivery, future milestone withdrawals will be reduced or excess ada will be returned.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in ADA price protection.

fact_verdicts.fact: 14. The anchor states deployment pauses if milestones are missed, reporting lapses, liquidity-health thresholds are breached, or material risks emerge, until the issue is resolved or governance provides direction.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in deployment pause rules.

fact_verdicts.fact: 15. The anchor states unused delivery funds, excess ada resulting from price protection, and profits/yield/returns generated by launch liquidity will be returned to the Treasury under the reporting process.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in refund and return conditions.

fact_verdicts.fact: 16. The anchor proposes Intersect as interim fund administrator, subject to confirmation and final agreement; if Intersect does not confirm within 30 days of enactment, the proposer will nominate an alternative independent administrator, subject to community notice and appropriate constitutional review.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in administration controls.

fact_verdicts.fact: 17. The anchor states any ada held by the administrator will be kept in auditable accounts and delegated to auto-abstain, not to a Stake Pool Operator.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the abstract and administration controls.

fact_verdicts.fact: 18. The anchor states Sundial remains accountable for controlled deployment of treasury-linked funds, compliance with use restrictions, reporting, coordination with Charms, and delivery of the funded scope.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in administration controls.

fact_verdicts.fact: 19. The anchor states Sundial will disclose related-party relationships and material commercial arrangements involving Sundial, Charms, or third parties connected to this proposal.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in administration controls.

fact_verdicts.fact: 20. The proposed system uses a shared BTC reserve architecture where reserve ratio is (V x P) / L, with V as BTC in the vault, P as BTC/USD price, and L as total ICE liability in USD; FIRE price is ((V x P) - L) / N+, where N+ is total FIRE supply.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the formulas and definitions in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 21. The anchor states the initial target reserve ratio is 5.0x; above 4.0x normal FIRE and ICE minting and redemption are enabled; between 2.0x and 4.0x new ICE minting and FIRE redemption are constrained; below 2.0x FIRE and ICE minting and redemption are constrained and new risky activity pauses.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the safety-zone description in the anchor rationale.

fact_verdicts.fact: 22. The anchor describes FIRE as the volatility-absorbing residual claim that receives upside from the shared reserve after ICE commitments are met and takes first-loss downside when BTC falls; it says FIRE creates amplified BTC exposure without liquidation mechanics or margin calls.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the FIRE description.

fact_verdicts.fact: 23. The anchor describes ICE as a USD-denominated BTC-backed asset designed for lower-volatility exposure with growth potential.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the ICE description.

fact_verdicts.fact: 24. The anchor says Alchemy will deliver open-source SDKs and adapters for wallets, DEXs, and DeFi protocols; dashboards showing reserve ratio, asset supply, liquidity health, fee flows, and deployment status; monthly governance reports; and reporting standards future BTCfi projects can adopt.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the public infrastructure section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 25. The anchor identifies Charms as technical partner and says Charms provides the foundation for FIRE and ICE to be issued with Bitcoin-native logic and circulate into Cardano wallets, DEXs, and DeFi tooling.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Charms technical partner section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 26. The anchor acknowledges Charms protocol-layer risk and says bridge, oracle, asset-accounting, or protocol vulnerability could impair reserve health; proposed mitigation includes independent security review, economic modeling, staged deployment, dashboard reporting, and deployment pause rules.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Charms risk paragraph.

fact_verdicts.fact: 27. The anchor lists main risks as BTC volatility, oracle risk, bridge risk, Charms protocol-layer risk, novel asset risk, regulatory treatment, delivery risk, and adoption risk.
fact_verdicts.note: Directly entailed by the main risks paragraph, which names those risk categories and regulatory treatment under novel asset risk.

fact_verdicts.fact: 28. The anchor states BTC volatility mitigation is a 5.0x target reserve ratio, hard safety zones, and staged deployment.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the main risks paragraph.

fact_verdicts.fact: 29. The anchor states oracle, bridge, and Charms protocol-layer risk mitigation is independent security review, economic modeling, staged launch, dashboards, and pause rules.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the main risks paragraph.

fact_verdicts.fact: 30. The anchor states novel asset risk mitigation is legal/compliance work, clear disclosures, and avoiding risk-free claims.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the main risks paragraph.

fact_verdicts.fact: 31. The anchor states delivery risk mitigation is milestone gating, fund separation, reporting, and administrator oversight.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the main risks paragraph.

fact_verdicts.fact: 32. The anchor states adoption risk mitigation is staged launch liquidity, integrations, go-to-market execution, and the rollover commitment.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the main risks paragraph.

fact_verdicts.fact: 33. On-chain recipient history states stake address stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr appears in 61 withdrawal proposals totalling 660216978000000 lovelace, first 2025-07-17 02:35:25 and last 2026-06-24 03:01:28.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 recipient history.

fact_verdicts.fact: 34. On-chain DRep vote counts so far are yes=8, no=16, abstain=8, explicit ballots and not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: Source Material 1 gives yes=8, no=17, abstain=8, but the source explicitly instructs that differing ballot counts are moving snapshots and must be treated as not_found, not contradicted.

fact_verdicts.fact: 35. Treasury flow over 36 epochs is inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 treasury flow.

fact_verdicts.fact: 36. BEACN doctrine ranks treasury stewardship and downside protection above evidence quality and reproducibility, public-benefit ecosystem growth, and execution speed/social consensus momentum.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the values hierarchy ordering.

fact_verdicts.fact: 37. BEACN doctrine says if ecosystem growth conflicts with treasury stewardship and evidence is weak, prefer ABSTAIN.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in conflict handling defaults.

fact_verdicts.fact: 38. BEACN doctrine says if treasury upside is plausible but non-verifiable, judge directionally on the available public record with a caution penalty, and in practice NO or ABSTAIN on thin asks.
fact_verdicts.note: Supported by values_hierarchy.md and treasury_spending_doctrine.md dossier gate posture.

fact_verdicts.fact: 39. BEACN doctrine rejects first-come-first-served budget models and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework rewarding proposal quality over submission timing.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the doctrine core position.

fact_verdicts.fact: 40. BEACN doctrine states available(e) = NCL_annual - Σ withdrawals(e - window_size ... e), with window_size = 73 epochs.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the rolling window equation and definitions.

fact_verdicts.fact: 41. BEACN doctrine states proposals within available budget are funded by DRep approval weight, descending, not by submission order.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the prioritization section.

verification.summary: All dossier facts are supported except Fact 34, which has a moving on-chain ballot-count mismatch and is therefore classified as not_found under the supplied audit instructions. No material discrepancies are listed.
